---

# AI engineering
Soniox offers AI tools integrated into your coding environment:

- **MCP server:** Access Soniox docs within Cursor, Windsurf, Claude Code, etc.
- **AI assistant:** Ask API questions, get code explanations, guidance directly.
- **LLM context files:** `llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` provide API and example context to AI models for better assistance.
- **Copy/Open buttons:** Copy page Markdown or open content in ChatGPT/Claude for live interactive help.

Setup MCP server with:

```json
"soniox-docs": {
  "command": "npx",
  "args": ["-y","mcp-remote","https://soniox.com/docs/api/mcp/mcp"]
}
```

More info & pricing at [soniox.com](https://soniox.com).

---

# Community and support
Support levels:

- **Free:** Developer community on [Discord](https://discord.gg/rWfnk9uM5j).
- **Business/Enterprise:** Dedicated support with SLAs; contact sales@soniox.com.

Track issues and find official code on [Soniox GitHub](https://github.com/soniox).

See [Soniox website](https://soniox.com/) for products & pricing.

---

# Data residency
- Your audio and transcript content **never used for training**.
- Enables choosing processing & storage **region per project** (US, EU, Japan).
- Using a regional project API key and regional domain ensures data stays local.
- System metadata (account, billing) may be processed outside the selected region.
- Contact sales@soniox.com to enable or request custom regions.

Regional endpoints:

| Region         | API domain                                                                       |
|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| United States  | `api.soniox.com`, `stt-rt.soniox.com`, `tts-rt.soniox.com`                      |
| European Union | `api.eu.soniox.com`, `stt-rt.eu.soniox.com`, `tts-rt.eu.soniox.com`             |
| Japan          | `api.jp.soniox.com`, `stt-rt.jp.soniox.com`, `tts-rt.jp.soniox.com`             |

---

# FAQ

**High WebSocket connection times (>1s) caused by:**

- Network latency or cross-region connections → Use regional endpoints.
- Large initial context payload → Send smaller context or buffer audio locally before connecting.

**For higher concurrent API rates:**

Request limit increase in Soniox Console; reviewed in 1-3 business days.

**Legal docs (MSA, DPA) available for Business/Enterprise customers:** contact sales@soniox.com.

---

# Introduction
Soniox provides APIs for:

- Speech-to-text and translation (60+ languages)
- Text-to-speech with natural voices (60+ languages)

Features:

- Real-time and batch APIs
- SDKs and WebSocket streaming support
- Self-service [Soniox Console](https://console.soniox.com) to manage keys, usage, logs, billing

Products:

- [Speech-to-Text](/stt/get-started)
- [Text-to-Speech](/tts/get-started)

---

# Security and privacy
- Compliance: SOC 2 Type 2, ISO/IEC 27001:2022, GDPR, HIPAA.
- Content (audio, transcripts) **never used to train models**.
- Data retention only if explicitly requested (e.g., async API).
- Logs minimal, no raw audio/transcripts, used for debugging and billing.
- All communication encrypted with TLS 1.2+.
- Stored data restricted by account.

---

# Errors
- REST API errors have structured JSON with stable `error_type`.
- Async transcription jobs may fail asynchronously; check transcription `status` and `error_type`.
- Common error types:

| error_type                     | HTTP | Cause & handling summary                                                                   |
|-------------------------------|------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `internal_error`               | 500  | Server error; retry once, then contact support with `request_id`.                           |
| `unauthenticated`             | 401  | Invalid or missing API key; provide valid Bearer token.                                   |
| `invalid_request`             | 400  | Validation errors; correct request data as per `validation_errors`.                        |
| `invalid_cursor`              | 400  | Malformed pagination cursor; start from first page or use last `next_page_cursor`.         |
| `limit_exceeded`              | 429  | Rate limits or quotas exceeded; slow down or request increased limits in Console.          |
| `organization_balance_exhausted` | 402  | No funds; top up account or enable autopay; failed async jobs must be resubmitted.         |
| `project_monthly_budget_exhausted` | 402  | Monthly budget reached; increase limit or wait for reset.                                 |
| `invalid_audio_file`          | 400  | Corrupt or unsupported audio; re-encode or shorten file.                                  |
| `file_download_blocked`       | N/A  | Async only. URL resolved to blocked IP; use reachable public URLs.                        |
| `file_download_failed`        | N/A  | Async only. Cannot fetch URL due to network error.                                        |
| `transcription_output_too_long` | N/A | Async only. Output too long for the model; contact support.                                |
| `model_not_available`         | 400  | Unknown or retired model; use current supported models.                                 |
| `file_not_found`              | 404  | File ID not found in project scope.                                                     |
| `transcription_not_found`     | 404  | Transcription ID not found in project scope.                                           |
| `transcription_invalid_state` | 409  | Transcript unavailable (e.g., processing, failed, deleted); check status before fetching. |

Realtime WebSocket and TTS REST API have their own error formats, see their respective docs.

---

# Soniox API reference

**Base URLs** vary by service and region; see [data residency](#data-residency).

## REST APIs

- Auth: Create temporary API keys (`POST /v1/auth/temporary-api-key`)
- STT: Files and transcriptions management, models listing
- TTS: Generate speech (`POST /tts`), models listing (`GET /v1/tts-models`)

## WebSocket APIs

- Speech-to-Text: `wss://stt-rt.soniox.com` 
- Text-to-Speech: `wss://tts-rt.soniox.com/tts-websocket`

Errors in REST have stable `error_type`; WebSocket errors return `error_code` and `error_message`.

---

# Soniox Live
Demo app streaming microphone audio directly to Soniox real-time API with live transcription & translation.

- Features: real-time low-latency speech recognition and translation in 60+ languages.
- Client: React (web), React Native (mobile).
- Server: Python to issue temporary API keys.
- Source code: [GitHub](https://github.com/soniox/soniox_examples/tree/master/apps/soniox-live-demo)

---

# Soniox Voice Agent
Demo app showing conversational AI assistant with streaming STT, LLM, and TTS.

- Example persona: Appointment booking for "Soniox AutoWorks".
- Client: React frontend, Twilio phone integration proxy.
- Server: Python modular processors (VAD, STT, LLM, TTS).
- Features: real-time, multilingual, customizable AI, multiple interaction methods.
- Source code: [GitHub](https://github.com/soniox/soniox_examples/tree/master/apps/soniox-voice-bot-demo)

---

# Direct stream
Low-latency client → Soniox STT WebSocket connection **without a proxy server**.

- Use temporary API keys limited for WebSocket STT usage (`usage_type: "transcribe_websocket"`).
- Example server endpoints provided in Python (FastAPI) and Node.js (Express) to issue temporary API keys.
- HTML client streams mic audio, connects to WebSocket API via temporary key, displays live transcription.
- Buffer audio locally while establishing connection to avoid gaps.
- Temporary API key request JSON example:

```json
{
  "usage_type": "transcribe_websocket",
  "expires_in_seconds": 60
}
```

---

# Proxy stream
Client streams audio to proxy WebSocket, proxy connects to Soniox STT WebSocket API.

- Proxy authenticates with main API key.
- Proxy forwards client audio and Soniox responses.
- Useful if you want to inspect, transform, or store audio/transcripts on server.
- Example proxy servers: Python (asyncio+websockets), Node.js (ws).
- Client example streams audio from mic via MediaRecorder to proxy WebSocket and shows live transcripts.
- Scheme:

Browser <-> Proxy Server <-> Soniox WebSocket

---

# Temporary API keys

Short-lived API keys for untrusted clients to directly connect to Soniox.

- Created server-side using long-lived API key; pass restrictions at creation.
- Fields:
  - `usage_type`: `"transcribe_websocket"` or `"tts_rt"`.
  - `expires_in_seconds`: max 3600 seconds.
  - `single_use`: restrict to 1 stream.
  - `max_session_duration_seconds`: duration limit per stream (up to 18000s).
  - `client_reference_id`: optional tracking ID (max 256 chars).

- Use temporary keys client-side to avoid exposing long-lived keys.
- Server creates temporary key via:

`POST /v1/auth/temporary-api-key`

Request body example:

```json
{
  "usage_type": "transcribe_websocket",
  "expires_in_seconds": 60,
  "single_use": true
}
```

- Behavior:
  - Expiration controls how long the key can open streams; open streams persist after expiry.
  - When session duration exceeded, connection closes with 403 error.
  - Speech-to-Text WebSocket returns JSON close message; TTS supports multi-stream and stream-level errors.
  - For REST TTS, error before audio → HTTP 403, after audio → HTTP Trailers.

---

# Usage logs

Per-request records of transcription or speech generation.

- Includes model, audio duration, token counts, cost, timestamps.
- Optional `client_reference_id` identifiers for end-user or session tracking.
- Can bind `client_reference_id` at temporary key creation (cannot be overridden by clients).
- Access logs via:

`GET /v1/usage-logs?start_time=<ISO>&end_time=<ISO>`

- Only completed successful requests logged.
- Supported APIs: STT WebSocket, STT Async, TTS WebSocket, TTS REST.

---

# Community integrations

- Agent Voice Response (AVR) for Asterisk PBX (https://github.com/agentvoiceresponse)
- Unofficial Go SDK (https://github.com/moxierobots/soniox-stt-go)

Note: These integrations are community-built, not officially supported.

---

# Integrations overview

Soniox integrates with platforms for real-time, multilingual voice apps:

- LiveKit
- Pipecat
- Vercel AI SDK
- TanStack AI SDK
- LangChain (Python and JS)
- Twilio
- n8n

Each integration offers components hooking Soniox STT/TTS into workflows in those platforms.

---

# n8n Integration

Soniox Node-based n8n node for speech-to-text in workflows.

- Supports audio sources: Binary files, Audio URLs, File IDs.
- Operations: Create transcription (sync or async via webhook), get results, delete resources.
- Language hints, speaker diarization, language identification, custom context, and translation options available.
- Supports webhook authentication headers and metadata.
- Auto-delete of transcription & uploaded files configurable.
- Basic to advanced usage covered with examples.
- Install via npm: `npm install @soniox/n8n-nodes-soniox`.

---

# TanStack AI SDK Integration

- Provides a Soniox transcription adapter for unified AI transcription interface.
- Install with `npm install @soniox/tanstack-ai-adapter`.
- Example: `generateTranscription({ adapter: sonioxTranscription('stt-async-v4'), audio: URL, modelOptions: {...} });`
- Options include language hints, context, diarization, translation.
- Access raw tokens with metadata via `providerMetadata`.
- Supports one-way and two-way translation.

---

# Twilio Integration

- Stream Twilio live call audio to Soniox real-time STT API.
- Receive real-time transcription via WebSockets.
- Complete example: https://github.com/soniox/soniox-twilio-realtime-transcription
- Steps: Create Twilio account, obtain Soniox API key, configure server and expose via ngrok, update TwiML to stream audio.
- Client connects to server WebSocket to display live transcription.
- Use provided Python call script to place test calls.

---

# Vercel AI SDK Integration

- Soniox transcription provider for Vercel AI SDK.
- Install `npm install @soniox/vercel-ai-sdk-provider`.
- Create provider with `createSoniox({ apiKey, apiBaseUrl })`.
- Use with `experimental_transcribe` function.
- Supports language hints, context, diarization, translation.
- See [Speech-to-Text API reference](/api-reference/stt/transcriptions/create_transcription).

---

# React Native SDK

- Use `@soniox/react` and `@soniox/client` to capture mic audio and stream real-time STT in React Native.
- Create custom `AudioSource` wrappers for audio capture libraries.
- Use hooks like `useRecording()` to manage sessions, get transcription text.
- Use temporary API keys to avoid exposing long-lived keys in client.
- Example with startup, start/stop controls, and rendering transcripts.
- Next steps: explore real-time transcription, speech generation, full SDK reference.

---

# Get started - Speech-to-Text

- Create Soniox account and get API key.
- Clone official examples from https://github.com/soniox/soniox_examples.
- Run real-time or async transcription examples in Python or Node.js.
- Features: languages hints, speaker diarization, real-time one-way and two-way translation.
- Async supports transcribing from URLs or uploaded files.
- Next steps: explore real-time API, async API, error handling.

---

# Get started - Text-to-Speech

- Create account and obtain API key.
- Clone and run real-time (WebSocket) and REST TTS examples (Python, Node.js).
- Generate speech in 60+ languages, multiple voices.
- Use real-time streaming TTS for low-latency voice agents; REST TTS for batch or server-side.
- Next steps: real-time API and REST API docs.

---

# Models

**Speech-to-Text Models**

- Current: `stt-rt-v4` (real-time), `stt-async-v4` (async)
- Aliases available for v3 versions
- Key improvements: higher accuracy, better multilingual handling, endpoint detection, longer audio processing.
- Deprecations: older preview models to be retired by Feb 2026.

**Text-to-Speech Models**

- Current: `tts-rt-v1`
- Aliases: preview points to v1
- Capabilities: native quality, hallucination-free, supports 60+ languages and multiple voices.

---

# API Endpoints Summaries

### Create temporary API key
`POST /v1/auth/temporary-api-key`

Request body (JSON):

- `usage_type` (string, required) - `transcribe_websocket` or `tts_rt`.
- `expires_in_seconds` (int, required) - 1 to 3600 seconds.
- `client_reference_id` (optional string, max 256).
- `single_use` (optional bool).
- `max_session_duration_seconds` (optional int, 1–18000).

Response: 201 Created with JSON `{ api_key, expires_at }`.

Errors: 400 invalid params, 401 unauthorized, 429 limits, 500 internal.

---

### Get usage logs
`GET /v1/usage-logs`

Query parameters:

- `start_time` (ISO8601, required)
- `end_time` (ISO8601, required)
- `limit` (optional)
- `sort` (optional)
- `cursor` (optional for pagination)

Response: 200 with array of usage logs including model, client_reference_id, tokens, cost, timestamps.

---

### Get models
`GET /v1/models`

Returns available STT models and attributes including languages, translation capabilities.

---

### STT WebSocket API
`wss://stt-rt.soniox.com/transcribe-websocket`

Client sends JSON config:

- `api_key`: your API key (long-lived or temporary).
- `model`: e.g., `stt-rt-v4`.
- `audio_format`: `auto` for container autodetect or raw formats.
- `num_channels`, `sample_rate`: required for raw formats.
- Optional language hints, context, speaker diarization, language identification.
- Optional translation config (`one_way` or `two_way`).

Stream audio as binary frames. Close stream with empty frame to end.

Server responses are JSON with tokens:

- `tokens`: array of token objects `{ text, start_ms?, end_ms?, confidence, is_final, speaker?, translation_status?, language?, ... }`
- `final_audio_proc_ms`, `total_audio_proc_ms`
- Final message: with `"finished": true`.

Error responses close connection with JSON error `{ error_code, error_message }`.

---

### Generate speech (REST API)
`POST https://tts-rt.soniox.com/tts`

Request JSON:

```json
{
  "model": "tts-rt-v1",
  "language": "en",
  "voice": "Adrian",
  "audio_format": "wav",
  "text": "Hello from Soniox Text-to-Speech.",
  "sample_rate": 24000,
  "bitrate": 128000,
  "client_reference_id": "some_internal_id"
}
```

Response: raw audio bytes with appropriate content-type. Mid-stream errors transmitted via HTTP trailers.

Errors: 400 Bad request, 401 unauthorized, 402 payment required, 408 timeout, 429 rate limited, 500 server error, 503 service unavailable.

---

### TTS WebSocket API
`wss://tts-rt.soniox.com/tts-websocket`

Client sends config JSON per stream:

- `api_key`: API key
- `stream_id`: client unique stream ID
- `model`
- `language`
- `voice`
- `audio_format`
- Optional `sample_rate`, `bitrate`, `client_reference_id`

Then send incremental text chunks JSON:

```json
{ "text": "Hello there,", "text_end": false, "stream_id": "stream-001" }
```

Close stream with chunk having `"text_end": true`.

Server sends base64 audio chunks per stream ID:

```json
{ "audio": "...base64...", "audio_end": false, "stream_id": "stream-001" }
```

Followed by:

- `"audio_end": true` : last audio chunk
- `"terminated": true`: stream completed

Errors are stream-scoped and do not close connection except on connection-level failures.

---

### REST STT API endpoints (summary)

- **Files management:**
  - `POST /v1/files`: Upload file (multipart/form-data; file required)
  - `GET /v1/files`: List files with paging
  - `GET /v1/files/count`: Get total file count per source
  - `GET /v1/files/{file_id}`: Get metadata for a file
  - `DELETE /v1/files/{file_id}`: Delete a file permanently

- **Transcriptions:**
  - `POST /v1/transcriptions`: Create a transcription job (from `audio_url` or `file_id` or upload file through SDK)
  - `GET /v1/transcriptions`: List transcriptions with paging
  - `GET /v1/transcriptions/count`: Get transcription counts
  - `GET /v1/transcriptions/{transcription_id}`: Get transcription details
  - `GET /v1/transcriptions/{transcription_id}/transcript`: Get full transcript with token metadata (only when status=completed)
  - `DELETE /v1/transcriptions/{transcription_id}`: Delete transcription (cannot delete while processing)

- Error responses via standard JSON error body with `error_type` and details.

---

# SDKs

## Node SDK
- Fully typed API client supporting REST and WebSocket STT and TTS.
- Supports env vars for configuring api keys, regions, URLs.
- Examples:

```ts
import { SonioxNodeClient } from "@soniox/node";

const client = new SonioxNodeClient();

await client.files.upload(buffer);
const transcription = await client.stt.transcribe({ model: "stt-async-v4", audio_url: "https://..." });
console.log(transcription.transcript?.text);
```

- Real-time usage:

```ts
const session = client.realtime.stt({ model: "stt-rt-v4" });
await session.connect();
session.on("result", (res) => console.log(res.tokens.map(t => t.text).join("")));
await session.sendStream(myAudioStream, {finish:true});
```
- TTS streaming with multiStream support, REST TTS calls.

## Python SDK
- Intuitive client for REST and WebSocket STT and TTS.
- Supports sync (`SonioxClient`) and async (`AsyncSonioxClient`) usage.
- Examples:

```python
client = SonioxClient()
transcription = client.stt.transcribe(audio_url="https://...", wait=True)
print(transcription.transcript.text)
```

Real-time TTS:

```python
config = RealtimeTTSConfig(...)
with client.realtime.tts.connect(config=config) as session:
    session.send_text_chunks(["Hello world"], text_end=True)
    audio = b"".join(session.receive_audio_chunks())
```

- Supports uploading files, polling transcriptions, deleting resources, and webhook handling.

## Web SDK (JavaScript/browser)
- Client for browser environment using WebSocket for real-time STT and TTS.
- Use temporary API keys via backend.
- High-level recording API via `client.realtime.record()`.
- Example:

```ts
const client = new SonioxClient({
  config: async () => {
    const res = await fetch("/tmp-key", { method: "POST" });
    return await res.json();
  },
});
const recording = client.realtime.record({ model: "stt-rt-v4" });
recording.on("result", res => console.log(res.tokens));
```

- TTS with `client.realtime.tts()`, `sendText`, and async iteration for audio playback.

## React SDK
- React hooks built on Web SDK.
- Use `SonioxProvider` for context or pass config.
- `useRecording` for real-time STT with reactive text state and controls.
- `useTts` hook for real-time TTS with streaming, text chunking, playback, and error handling.
- Supports permissions and audio level metering.

---

# Core Concepts

## Confidence scores
Every recognized token includes a confidence score 0.0-1.0 indicating recognition likelihood.

---

## Context
Provide structured context to improve transcription and translation:

- `general`: key-value pairs for domain, participants, topic etc.
- `text`: long-form background text (notes, docs).
- `terms`: domain-specific or uncommon words.
- `translation_terms`: custom source-to-target translations.

Max context ~8,000 tokens.

---

## Language hints
Bias model toward expected languages via ISO codes (e.g., `["en","es"]`). Does not restrict recognition, just biases.

---

## Language identification
Each token includes detected language code when enabled. Works in streaming and async modes. Enables detecting mid-sentence language changes.

---

## Language restrictions
Strict mode biases output to only specified languages with `language_hints_strict: true`. Recommended to use a single language only for best results.

---

## Speaker diarization
Model labels tokens with speaker IDs when enabled for multi-speaker audio. Async mode is more accurate than real-time.

---

## Timestamps
Tokens have precise start and end millisecond offsets for aligning text with audio.

---

## Audio formats (TTS)
Supported output audio formats:

- Raw PCM: `pcm_s16le`, `pcm_f32le`, `pcm_mulaw`, `pcm_alaw`, `wav`
- Lossless compressed: `flac`
- Lossy compressed: `mp3`, `opus`, `aac`

Sample rates and bitrates vary by format; see detailed tables.

---

## Language mixing (TTS)
Single language per TTS request/session. To mix languages, split text and generate audio per language, then concatenate.

---

## Voices (TTS)
12 studio-quality voices available, usable across all 60+ supported languages with consistent identity.

---

# Guides & Patterns

### Async transcription
Upload file or submit public URL/file ID to `POST /v1/transcriptions`.

Optionally wait synchronously or use webhook for completion notifications.

Poll transcription status or get full transcript including tokens.

Clean up uploaded files and transcriptions manually or with `cleanup` options.

---

### Webhooks
Configure webhook URL and optional auth headers in transcription requests.

Receive POST callback on completion/errors with status and transcription ID.

Secure webhook with custom auth headers.

---

### Real-time transcription
Connect via WebSocket, send config, then audio frames.

Receive streaming JSON responses with partial and final tokens.

Use endpoint detection or manual finalization to detect speech turn ends.

Support for speaker diarization, language hints, translation.

---

### Real-time TTS
Connect via WebSocket, send config per stream, and incremental text.

Receive base64 audio chunks interleaved with text input.

Handle stream termination and cancellation with three-step handshake.

Supports multiple concurrent streams on one WebSocket connection.

---

### Temporary API keys lifecycle, keepalive, and error handling
Use server-issued short-lived, limited scope API keys for client connections.

Send keepalive messages every ~20 seconds (STT) or as required (TTS).

Handle API errors gracefully, with retries or fallback.

---

# Additional concepts

- **Direct stream:** Client connects directly to Soniox WebSocket with temp keys for lowest latency.
- **Proxy stream:** Client streams audio via backend proxy to Soniox WebSocket (adds inspect/transform layer).
- **SDK event model:** All realtime transcriptions and speech generations emit events (result, error, endpoint, finished).
- **Utterance buffers:** Aggregate tokens into logical utterances based on endpoint events.
- **Multi-stream TTS:** Single WebSocket connection can carry 5 concurrent TTS streams multiplexed by stream_id.

---

# Code Examples

**Python Async Transcribe from URL:**

```python
from soniox import SonioxClient

client = SonioxClient()
transcription = client.stt.transcribe(
    model="stt-async-v4",
    audio_url="https://soniox.com/media/examples/coffee_shop.mp3",
    wait=True,
)
transcript = client.stt.get_transcript(transcription.id)
print(transcript.text)
```

**Node real-time STT:**

```ts
const { SonioxNodeClient } = require("@soniox/node");
const client = new SonioxNodeClient();
const session = client.realtime.stt({ model: "stt-rt-v4" });
session.on("result", res => console.log(res.tokens.map(t => t.text).join("")));
await session.connect();
await session.sendStream(audioStream, { finish: true });
```

**Web Client direct stream HTML:**

```html
<button id="trigger">Start</button>
<script type="module">
  import { RecordTranscribe } from "https://unpkg.com/@soniox/speech-to-text-web?module";

  // On button click: fetch temporary key, create RecordTranscribe, start streaming mic audio
</script>
```

**TTS REST generation with Node SDK:**

```ts
const client = new SonioxNodeClient();
const bytes = await client.tts.generateToFile("hello.wav", {
  text: "Hello from Soniox text-to-speech.",
  voice: "Adrian",
  model: "tts-rt-v1",
  language: "en",
  audio_format: "wav",
});
console.log(`Wrote ${bytes} bytes`);
```

**WebSocket TTS stream in Python (sync):**

```python
from soniox import SonioxClient, RealtimeTTSConfig
client = SonioxClient()
config = RealtimeTTSConfig(stream_id="...", model="tts-rt-v1", language="en", voice="Adrian", audio_format="wav")

with client.realtime.tts.connect(config=config) as session:
    session.send_text_chunk("Hello", text_end=True)
    audio = b"".join(session.receive_audio_chunks())
with open("output.wav", "wb") as f:
    f.write(audio)
```

---

# Summary

Soniox documentation covers:

- Powerful, production-ready STT and TTS APIs supporting real-time and asynchronous workflows.
- Comprehensive error handling and API reference.
- Support for 60+ languages with advanced features: language hinting/restriction, speaker diarization, context customization, real-time translation.
- Temporary API keys for secure client usage.
- Integration with major AI platforms and SDKs.
- Demo apps and guides for direct and proxy streaming.
- Official Node.js, Python, Web, and React SDKs with detailed usage and event-driven APIs.

Use the official SDKs and APIs to build fast, high-quality voice integrations with minimal effort and full control.

For detailed API schemas, code examples, and error definitions, refer to individual sections above and the [Soniox API reference](#soniox-api-reference).

---

For questions or help integrating Soniox, use the AI assistant built into the docs or contact support@soniox.com.

## Soniox website summary:

Soniox Website Summary

---

**Voice Platform**  
Soniox provides a unified real-time multilingual voice AI platform with speech-to-text (STT), text-to-speech (TTS), and translation in 60+ languages. Native-speaker accuracy supports mixed-language conversations, low-latency streaming (<200ms), speaker diarization, accurate alphanumeric recognition, and enterprise-grade compliance (SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR). One global API supports regional deployments (US, EU, Japan; more regions coming). Ideal for voice agents, meetings, call centers, and large-scale workflows.  
- API integration example available  
- Explore API & docs: https://soniox.com/docs  
- Compare with other providers live: https://soniox.com/compare  

---

**Soniox App**  
All-in-one voice workspace for individuals and teams. Features:  
- Smart Scribe: live transcription, meeting summaries, speaker separation  
- Translator: real-time speech translation across 60+ languages, supports language switching mid-sentence  
- Voice Typing: system-wide dictation in 60+ languages, up to 4× faster than typing  
Available on iOS, Android, macOS, Windows.  
- iOS App: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/soniox/id1560199731  
- Android App: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.soniox.sonioxmobileapp  
- Desktop apps for macOS/Windows: https://soniox.com/app  
Privacy: Audio is processed in real-time, never stored or used for training. Free to start with weekly free credits.  
- Support & docs: https://soniox.com/docs  

---

**Soniox Text-to-Speech**  
High-fidelity, hallucination-free TTS in 60+ languages with:  
- Accurate pronunciation of names, alphanumerics, foreign words  
- Language switching mid-sentence  
- Ultra-low-latency streaming that starts output before full sentence is available  
Built for voice agents, IVR, accessibility, media production, multilingual experiences.  
Pricing starts at $0.70/hour generated speech.  

---

**Benchmarks & Recognition**  
- Soniox achieves best-in-class STT accuracy and speed (1.29% WER; ~249ms latency in English) and native accuracy across 60+ languages including mixed language and noisy environments.  
- Supports real-time multi-speaker diarization and semantic end-point detection.  
- Data processed in-region complying with privacy and regulatory requirements.  

---

**Developer SDKs**  
Official SDKs for Python, Node.js, Web, React, React Native simplify integration with event-driven real-time and batch transcription; include utilities for language detection, speaker segmentation, and error handling.  

---

**Use Cases**  
- Voice Agents: Low-latency, accurate speech recognition and natural speech generation  
- Contact Centers: Real-time transcription and translation  
- Medical Transcription: Context-aware, domain-specific speech understanding  
- Media: Fast and accurate captioning and translation  
- Wearables: Streaming voice experiences with minimal delay  
- Multilingual communication & global voice products  

---

**Regional Data & Compliance**  
Soniox Sovereign Cloud enables deployment in US, EU, Japan, Korea, Australia, Canada, India, and more, with data residency and compliance with GDPR, HIPAA, SOC2, ISO 27001 standards.  

---

**Contact & Support**  
- General: info@soniox.com  
- Support: support@soniox.com or Discord: https://discord.gg/rWfnk9uM5j  
- Offices: Foster City, CA, USA & Ljubljana, Slovenia  

---

**Additional Info**  
- Soniox is independent and not affiliated with companies named "Sonix"  
- Pricing: Pay-as-you-use token-based with STT approx. $0.10-$0.12/hr, TTS approx. $0.70/hr  
- UGC Creator Program for Soniox App content creators available  
- Full language support list and voice agent demos for 60+ languages available at https://soniox.com  

---
